title: Codecov Integration
reviewedCommits: 84311d6 ci(codecov): add coverage upload step to CI workflow
strengths: All 7 packages are listed in the files: field, matching the monorepo exactly (core, graph, cli, eslint-plugin, linter-gen, orchestrator, types)
Upload step is correctly gated to ubuntu-latest only, consistent with the test:ci coverage guard
Step ordering is correct: test:ci -> coverage-ratchet -> codecov upload -> test:platform-parity. Coverage files exist before upload reads them
CODECOV_TOKEN is properly referenced via secrets -- no hardcoded tokens, no leaks in any workflow or env files
codecov.yml project target: auto with 1% threshold is a pragmatic baseline that prevents regressions without blocking on noise
Patch target of 80% is a reasonable bar for new code
flags.unittests.paths scoped to packages/ correctly limits coverage to workspace source

findings.title: codecov-action pinned to major tag, not SHA
findings.rationale: uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4 pins to a mutable major version tag. A compromised tag could inject malicious code into CI. Pinning to a full commit SHA (e.g., codecov/codecov-action@<sha>) with a version comment is the supply-chain-hardened approach. This is low risk given Codecov's prevalence but worth noting for defense-in-depth.
findings.suggestion: Pin to a specific SHA: uses: codecov/codecov-action@<sha> # v4.x.x. The SHA can be found from the Codecov action releases page.

findings.title: fail_ci_if_error: false means Codecov failures are silent
findings.rationale: With fail_ci_if_error: false, a misconfigured token, network error, or Codecov outage will not surface in CI. This is a reasonable default to avoid blocking PRs on a third-party service, but the team should be aware that upload failures will be invisible unless Codecov dashboards are monitored.
findings.suggestion: No change needed if this is intentional. Consider setting to true once the integration is stable and the CODECOV_TOKEN secret is confirmed provisioned.

findings.title: No explicit PR comment configuration in codecov.yml
findings.rationale: The codecov.yml does not include a comment: section. Codecov defaults to posting PR comments with coverage diffs. This is usually desirable but if the team wants to suppress or customize PR comment behavior, it should be configured explicitly.
findings.suggestion: If PR comments are desired (likely), no action needed. To customize: add a comment: section with layout, behavior, and require_changes_to_be_covered options.
evidence: codecov.yml has coverage.status and coverage.flags but no comment: block
